emails.subject: Welcome to NYWIFT, Lydia!
emails.body:
Dear Lydia,

Congratulations and Welcome to New York Women in Film & Television!

You've been given a 1-year complimentary membership. Your membership will be active through October 22, 2026.

Please respond back to this email with your full mailing address, phone number and preferred email address (if this is not it).

You may begin to take advantage of your membership with NYWIFT by signing into the Members Portal section of the website: nywift.org<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.nywift.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TlemRUsKAtMeLOn1MsmjJOWfF12l3mD95pK15sB4sTPqOtaVbUQyUy4KnV9G0TBbv11cwzBXmg6u2VHrYjcW5QTfyGU$ > (log-in located in the top-left corner.)

Username: chilton@cs.columbia.edu
Password: ColumUse5?

After logging in, you can change the password to whatever you like by clicking on 'Update Profile' on the left side of the screen. Please be sure to add your contact information and your resume with only industry-related projects here as well.

Don't forget to check out the other 'Membership Benefits' located in the same section – right above 'Print Member Card.'

Please check out NYWIFT's committees. Participating in a committee is a wonderful way to meet and work with your fellow dynamic members of NYWIFT. Committees form the engine of our association and play an important role in achieving an effective, progressive organization. There's one for every taste and we encourage you to join. If you don’t see one to your liking, consider reaching out to us to form a member affinity group of your own! For details about each committee, visit: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.nywift.org/committees/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TlemRUsKAtMeLOn1MsmjJOWfF12l3mD95pK15sB4sTPqOtaVbUQyUy4KnV9G0TBbv11cwzBXmg6u2VHrYjcWY6zxpCY$ 

If you have any questions regarding your membership, please contact: membership@nywift.org

Welcome aboard!

Al Dawkins (they/them)
Membership Assistant
New York Women in Film & Television<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.nywift.org__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TlemRUsKAtMeLOn1MsmjJOWfF12l3mD95pK15sB4sTPqOtaVbUQyUy4KnV9G0TBbv11cwzBXmg6u2VHrYjcWIOfsxvw$ >

emails.subject: DSI Undergraduate Data Science and AI Research Fair 2025
emails.body:
Dear colleagues,

Although this is a very busy time of the semester, we wanted to let you
know that *we have extended the deadline for undergraduates to submit their
research to this year's DSI research fair until this coming Monday at
midnight.*

This undergraduate student-led and -centered event is now in its
fourth year, and continues to be a unique opportunity for students to
present both their independent work and projects they have done for classes
or with faculty.

We would love it if you would help us spread the word; and if you or your
department would like to be more involved, we would welcome your
participation as publicity sponsors, submission reviewers and/or on-site
judges!

Please find details below, and don't hesitate to reach out with any
questions.

All best,

Susan McGregor & Eugene Wu

Co-Chairs, DSI Center for Data, Media & Society

------------------

*Deadline Extended - Call for Research Projects: **DSI Undergraduate Data
Science and AI Research Fair 2025*

AI is now embedded in classrooms, workplaces, and daily life, raising
questions about the kind of future we want to create. This year’s theme— Future
Forward: AI and the Next Decade
<https://datascience.columbia.edu/events/undergraduate-research-fair-2025/ >
—invites
undergraduate students to imagine lasting impacts of AI through posters,
demonstrations, and creative projects that explore its opportunities,
risks, and limits.

Students who participate will be eligible for a range of prizes and receive
feedback on their work from faculty, alumni, and industry attendees.

*Application Deadline - Extended:* Monday, October 27 (11:59 PM ET)

*APPLICATION FORM*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://form.jotform.com/DSI_Events/fair-application-2025__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Um8aV7tddQ2nREp0gS680pAIY0m8i3-zmLpKhiUPQcXL7ydLqtKB3xiF48YZ_FrjLJrZKnvP9gDc_W3GpfL1Ja1E46KJJym70g$ >

*Application Details:*

   - *Eligibility:* Open to all Columbia and Barnard undergraduates. Class
   projects are welcome but not required; independent projects without a
   faculty advisor are encouraged; works in progress are welcome.
   - *2025 Tracks (choose one in your application):* AI in Society: Human
   Futures (work, culture, politics, everyday life); Responsible & Sustainable
   AI (environment, security/privacy, transparency, governance); AI for
   Discovery & Innovation(research advances, domain applications, new models
   and methods)
   - *Formats Accepted:* Posters, table-based demos & visualizations,
   startup/entrepreneurial projects, and creative tech installations or
   performances.

The fair will be held on *Thursday, November 6 from 5:00 PM - 7:30 PM* in
Carleton Commons, Mudd Building. The event is hosted by the Data, Media,
and Society Center
<https://datascience.columbia.edu/research/centers/data-media-and-society/ >
at the Data Science Institute, co-organized by a committee of undergraduate
students across Columbia University and Barnard College, and co-sponsored
by schools and units across our campus. The event is free and open to all
to register and attend
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.eventbrite.com/e/columbia-undergraduate-data-science-and-ai-research-fair-tickets-1685635875279?aff=oddtdtcreator__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Um8aV7tddQ2nREp0gS680pAIY0m8i3-zmLpKhiUPQcXL7ydLqtKB3xiF48YZ_FrjLJrZKnvP9gDc_W3GpfL1Ja1E46JXL3-J_w$ >
.

Subject: AI Agents for Work *Schedule* available now

Following the success of the AI Agents for Work workshop in March, DAPLab<https://daplab.cs.columbia.edu/ > will host another workshop for industry leaders to present solutions and core challenges for transforming businesses with AI.

The full schedule is available now on the website:

https://daplab.cs.columbia.edu/workshop/2025-10/ 

Keynotes will be given by:

Sasha Rush from Cursor

Laurens van der Maaten from Anthropic

The workshop will take place at the Columbia Business School on Monday, October 27, 2025, from 9am to 5pm. Topics of focus will include:

  *   Trustworthy Proactive AI

  *   Continual Improvement of Agentic Systems

  *   Safety and Security

  *   MCP & Context Engineering

  *   Digital Twins

We will have industry talks from AI leaders at Anthropic, Cursor, MongoDB, Databricks, among many others.

If you or other AI experts at your company are interested in joining us, please register here<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.eventbrite.com/e/ai-agents-for-work-oct-2025-tickets-1636311284099?aff=oddtdtcreator__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Wq4LCxHmmJ52TFQvIznuICf-DAhIG6EpTO4R9XwmJpsfbNAdb8YNtLiurjtfiidvDJOiIHFtHaLPXtBGBQFAFLG8XA$> before space runs out. If you have more than five people in your company or organization who are potentially interested, please reach out, and we will do our best to accommodate them.

Registration is free. Breakfast and lunch are included. Parking is available.

https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.eventbrite.com/e/ai-agents-for-work-oct-2025-tickets-1636311284099?aff=oddtdtcreator__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!V4MkwbZ29Ce8R_fAmTY-yzq79zVRvCCeunOe0y9zIoBUh2wxWF4Fe8pH9dTejeJaSzKt_KlAp7BoPfBdJjR5dByL0A$ <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.eventbrite.com/e/ai-agents-for-work-oct-2025-tickets-1636311284099?aff=oddtdtcreator__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Wq4LCxHmmJ52TFQvIznuICf-DAhIG6EpTO4R9XwmJpsfbNAdb8YNtLiurjtfiidvDJOiIHFtHaLPXtBGBQFAFLG8XA$>

See you on October 27!

Eugene Wu, Zhou Yu, and Lydia Chilton

DAPLab Workshop organizers, Columbia CS Faculty

emails.subject: Lori Glover named DSI Chief of Strategic Alliances
emails.body:
Dear Colleagues,

I’m thrilled to announce that Lori Glover will join Columbia’s Data Science
Institute as Chief of Strategic Alliances, a new role in which she will
lead industry and research partnerships through the Data Science Institute,
involving faculty across Columbia University. Lori recently authored Innovation
Alchemy <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://innovationalchemy.net/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SZNhbNB5se2mSz0nNWkEdo3uZk8Gv_0Xd-h9r5sDuajRFet3hdBc14NUqsThRJkmzB1NYTHtb4avbVO9sc6rjnv6t9ir7PIkQuLV_Q$ >, a practical playbook for building
meaningful academic-industry alliances. Lori is most excited about building
a partnership ecosystem that leverages Columbia’s disciplinary strengths
across all of our schools.

Lori comes to Columbia after more than a decade at MIT, where she built the
Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory’s (CSAIL) Alliances
program from the ground up. She has created one of the most successful
university-industry partnership programs in the country, which includes
focused efforts on FinTechAI@CSAIL, Machine Learning Applications@CSAIL,
and the MIT Future of Data, Trust & Privacy.  The CSAIL Alliances program
currently engages more than 150 companies globally and supports a broad
portfolio of research, talent, and innovation collaborations at MIT’s
largest lab, home to more than 900 active projects.

Lori will join us in January 2026. We will be hosting an opportunity to
meet with Lori in early December - I hope you will all join me in welcoming
Lori to the University.

Warmly,

Garud

Garud N. Iyengar

Avanessians Director of the Data Science Institute

Professor of Industrial Engineering and Operations Research

Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science

On 10/13/2025 4:58 PM, Lydia Chilton wrote:

Hi Shih-Fu,

Perhaps you are aware that DAPLab is running the second Agents at Work
Workshop on Monday, Oct 27, at CBS (with a special partners-only workshop
on October 28).

You were kind enough to introduce the event last year, if you're free, we'd
love to have you do an introduction again - either morning or afternoon (or
anytime that suits you)

All the information is below. We'd love to have you if you're available.

Cheers,
Lydia, Eugene, Zhou

----

Following the success of the AI Agents for Work workshop
<https://daplab.cs.columbia.edu/workshop/2025-03/ > in March, DAPLab
<https://daplab.cs.columbia.edu/ > will be running another workshop for
industry leaders in AI agents to present solutions and unearth core
challenges.

The workshop will take place at the Columbia Business School on Monday
October 27, 2025, from 9am to 5pm. Topics of focus will include:

   -

   Trustworthy Proactive AI
   -

   Continual Improvement of Agentic Systems
   -

   Safety and Security
   -

   MCP & Context Engineering
   -

   Digital Twins

We will have industry talks from AI leaders at Anthropic, Cursor, MongoDB,
Databricks, among many others.

If you or other AI experts at your company are interested in joining us,
please register here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.eventbrite.com/e/ai-agents-for-work-oct-2025-tickets-1636311284099?aff=oddtdtcreator__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!V1WGyEM0tgI608FNo3iT4mgWGEkZeKOxJVLF3D--AzFMguZ3LH1uRSs_O08auYQSdTkZrDiZId1X50-mpaqAJwU48g$>
before
space runs out. (Last March, we ran out of all 200 tickets a week before
the event). If you have more than 5 people in your company or organization
who are potentially interested, reach out, and we will try to accommodate.

Registration is free. Breakfast and lunch are included. Parking is
available.

AWS Cryptography
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.amazon.science/research-awards/call-for-proposals/aws-cryptography-call-for-proposals-fall-2025__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VC7XA6K-GZEvOWEorAN54R8V363WW_8pWsp6y1JLX9S2f6V-SHbrUsLZG4MaaENpp7-oWML8ZbWy5ZbHLrC8-mdM$>,

Sustainability
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.amazon.science/research-awards/call-for-proposals/sustainability-call-for-proposals-fall-2025__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VC7XA6K-GZEvOWEorAN54R8V363WW_8pWsp6y1JLX9S2f6V-SHbrUsLZG4MaaENpp7-oWML8ZbWy5ZbHLiWg8qnC$>,
and Think Big
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.amazon.science/research-awards/call-for-proposals/think-big-call-for-proposals-fall-2025__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VC7XA6K-GZEvOWEorAN54R8V363WW_8pWsp6y1JLX9S2f6V-SHbrUsLZG4MaaENpp7-oWML8ZbWy5ZbHLip9-Ywa$>
research areas. The deadline for submissions is 11:59 p.m. PT on November
5, 2025.

Proposals should be a maximum of 3 pages (4 for Think Big), not including
appendices. Templates for each CFP can be found in the links above. Proposals
will be reviewed for the quality of their scientific content, creativity,
and their potential for impact at scale. Proposals related to theory,
practice, and novel new techniques are all welcome.

ARA provides grant recipients unrestricted funds, ranging from $70K-$100K,
and AWS promotional credits, ranging from $40K-$70K, $250K for Build on
Training CFP. Funded projects are assigned an Amazon research contact, and
recipients also receive training resources, including AWS tutorials and
hands-on sessions with Amazon scientists and engineers.

<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.amazon.science_research-2Dawards&d=DwMFAg&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IiMYPvHHzEdY1iyRRF_TTFtN2M8aY3xAjEytEwU1O6k&m=4dyLxFxpJtTfZYhcuMSCDRQEDEyo4gY6klSx216vuYGU_bb1C8JhoIbw8YnSUAaP&s=tQGYZC6IacqtWt9-D6RulizTXnmQIJxwZnM9nP0_w2M&e= >

>> wrote:
>>
>>> I spent some time talking with Carl and figuring out how we could work
>>> things so that there is less pressure on him while he's on sabbatical.
>>> Given that discussion, he agreed to stay on. So I think we're back to that
>>> original team. Basically, while on sabbatical  i gave him freedom from
>>> meetings and the demo (we will do text only demo in the 250K period and I
>>> think that's fine).
>>>
>>> He will not come to the meeting with Colin because he's worked with him
>>> before and he's fine with having him on the team.
>>>
>>> Following the meeting with Colin, we should stay on the call to make
>>> sure we're as a group good with going with Colin. Also, that we can include
>>> the other organizations (e.g., NewsStory and the lab at Penn as external
>>> organizations with whom we will do for example user testing). We will not
>>> give them funding, although I do think we can pay for user studies and so
>>> they might end up getting some funding that way.
>>>
>>> Also, on the document itself, I definitely need input and I need this
>>> kind of input:
>>>
>>> 1. JOHN - Can you look at the description of what we plan to do. I wrote
>>> this based on our discussion and Carl's description. You felt a connection
>>> with this and understood what it would look like. Can you check what I
>>> wrote and see if I got it right? Are there things you would change there?
>>>
>>> 2. LYDIA - I need something more concrete to include about what you will
>>> do. For everyone else, we have been talking about it. I imagine you doing
>>> two things: 1. human in the loop and 2. human subjects (perhaps with Eric
>>> Feng or others) to measure whether our approach and theory is successful.
>>> Can you add AT LEAST TWO SENTENCES ON THIS. POSSIBLY THREE?
>>>
>>> 3.JOHN - For you I put in a. phrase about interpretability and
>>> explanation. If this is not what you will do and you think you will do
>>> something else, you can change and/or add.
>>>
>>> 3. SMARA - I know you will become available on Thursday. CAN YOU HELP ME
>>> WITH THE LANGUAGE. I don't think language matters that much on the abstract
>>> because we are not selling ourselves here, but nonetheless don't want to be
>>> embarassed by our submission. I will work on this also. I believe the
>>> language needs to be more forceful to say why we are so great and why what
>>> we are doing is novel and important. LYDIA AND JOHN - you are welcome to
>>> help with this also if you want.
>>>
>>> Thanks! And see you at 4:30.
>>>
>>> Kathy
>>>
>>>
>>> -------------------------

emails.subject: Re: Meta + Columbia Workshop Round 2
emails.body:
Hi Lydia,

We’ll miss you! Thanks for letting me know. Hope to have you next time.

Best,
Hortense

From: Lydia Chilton <chilton@cs.columbia.edu>
Date: Wednesday, October 22, 2025 at 10:47 AM
To: Fong, Hortense <hf2462@gsb.columbia.edu>
Subject: Re: Meta + Columbia Workshop Round 2
Hi Hortense! I’m so glad you’re doing the workshop again with Meta. It was fantastic last year!

I’m unfortunately teaching a workshop 9-2 that day. I’m very been trying to move it… but I don’t think it will be possible.

I’m so sad to miss this!but hopefully there will be a next time:)
Lydia

On Wed, Oct 15, 2025 at 18:32 Fong, Hortense <hf2462@gsb.columbia.edu<mailto:hf2462@gsb.columbia.edu>> wrote:
Hi Lydia,

Hope all is well. We are planning another Meta + Columbia workshop on Friday, Nov 7 and I am wondering if you would have any interest in presenting again this year. Everyone really enjoyed hearing about your work last year, and it would be great to have you again. The structure is similar to last year’s and the goal is the same: to bring together academics and practitioners to discuss different problems each group faces, with an eye towards potential collaboration opportunities. Please let me know if this is something you would be interested in.

Best,
Hortense

emails.body:
Hey Lydia,

It was a fun event today – actually, it was the discussion on our way back that was more provoking for me. Really appreciate the insights shared by you and Eugene! It’s baking in my head…

Back to the point about adding my name to the DAP Lab people page<https://daplab.cs.columbia.edu/people >, this is really low priority, but since folks at some startup asked me… Would love to be added to this page if appropriate. Attaching my headshot (which is a couple of years old…).

And just asked one of my students to submit their agent-related poster. I assume they will only attend the Day 1, but if you think it’s helpful to have them on Day 2, I am happy to ask.

Best,

emails.subject: Dr. Dennis A. Mitchell Appointed Dean of Columbia University College of Dental Medicine
emails.body:
October 22, 2025
Dear members of the Columbia community,

We are pleased to share the appointment of Dennis A. Mitchell as the next Dean of the Columbia University College of Dental Medicine and Senior Vice President, Columbia University Irving Medical Center. Dr. Mitchell has been an invaluable leader at this institution for the last 34 years, and since February 2025, he has served as Interim Dean of the College of Dental Medicine. We are very grateful that he will take on the role of Dean in a permanent capacity. 

A renowned clinician, researcher, and administrator, Dr. Mitchell joined the Columbia faculty in 1991 and has since devoted much of his career to strengthening both the faculty and student experience and fostering an inclusive campus climate. Dennis is Professor of Dental Medicine (Community Health and Periodontics) at CUIMC and recently served as Senior Advisor for Inclusion and Belonging. His impact can be felt across the University through the numerous administrative roles that he has held, including as Executive Vice President for University Life, Interim Provost, and Senior Vice Provost for Faculty Advancement.

As one of the nation’s leading dental administrators, Dennis has long been dedicated to training and shaping future generations of leaders and professionals in the field. His academic work has focused on the oral manifestations of HIV, the treatment of periodontal disease and the risk of preterm birth, and the oral health needs of underserved populations. He co-created and served as Director of the Community DentCare Network for Columbia for a decade. He also previously served as Director of Research and Community Dentistry at Harlem Hospital.

After joining Columbia’s Office of the Provost in 2014, Dr. Mitchell was responsible for overseeing the University’s ongoing commitment to recruit and retain faculty with diverse experiences and backgrounds. This work included the creation and enhancement of faculty development and support resources and programs to develop pathways to the professoriate. 

Dennis has been widely recognized for his contributions to the fields of dental medicine and public health. He is the 2025 recipient of the ADEA Gies Award for Eminence in Achievement. He is a fellow in the American College of Dentists and received the American Dental Education Association Distinguished Service Award in 2022. In 2021, he was named a fellow in the Academy of Community and Public Health Service at CUIMC. Dr. Mitchell earned his DDS from the Howard University College of Dentistry and completed his general dentistry residency training at Harlem Hospital. He obtained his MPH degree in executive health services management at the Columbia Mailman School of Public Health. 

We can think of no one more qualified to serve as the next Dean of the College of Dental Medicine. Please join us in thanking Lorraine Frazier, Dean of the School of Nursing and the Mary O’Neil Mundinger Professor of Nursing, and the search committee for their thoughtful work and in congratulating Dennis on his much-deserved new role.

Regards,

Claire Shipman

Acting President, Columbia University in the City of New York

Katrina Armstrong, MD

Dean of the Faculties of Health Sciences and the Vagelos College of Physicians and Surgeons

Executive Vice President for Health and Biomedical Sciences, Columbia University

Copyright (c) 2025 Columbia University. All rights reserved.You are receiving this email because you are part of the Columbia University community.

Columbia University

 116th and Broadway

 New York, NY 10027

emails.subject: Laude Moonshot grant submission
emails.body:
Hey Lydia (cc'ed Rich),

It was great to finally meet you this morning. We're excited to have you on
board for the Laude Moonshot proposal. My impression was that there
is broad interest in engaging business scholars in shaping the impact of AI
in the workplace. I started drafting a (very) rough proposal here as a
conversation-starter:

https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wQhn9LUqb7mSgWrdzjh5dcQxc_rpZcMGuOQitxkjsRc/edit?usp=sharing__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WekeiQyeW7qG8kP4q_4BO0wMQUSk2YOJ5ECApBCrVQWIJmXVF1QufVZACyIE8m02AdS4jZM6P7BnPS_8r3TCOraI$ 

You can find the 1-page version at the end.

Timeline

   - The 1-page abstract deadline is *Oct 25. *I don't think this is that
   important—it's mostly for gauging demand to set up their review process.
   - We need to submit a 4-page proposal by *Nov 25*. They will pick 8
   teams to award $250K. *We're meeting next week in Kravis to
   discuss concrete next steps for the proposal.*
   - If we get chosen, we need to deliver initial results in 6 months for a
   chance to get a $10M lab.
   - If you're interested, let's schedule a brief meeting this week. Since
   the 1-page abstract is not that important, we can start iterating on the
   Google doc asynchronously.

Background
Before you go over the draft, the following background may be useful for
grounding:

   - Employment section in this position paper
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.02730__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WekeiQyeW7qG8kP4q_4BO0wMQUSk2YOJ5ECApBCrVQWIJmXVF1QufVZACyIE8m02AdS4jZM6P7BnPS_8r-zKjGeP$ > that started the funding agency
   - Slide from Laude's webinar

   - Blog post from Mercor
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://mercor.com/blog/the-economy-will-become-an-rl-environment-machine/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WekeiQyeW7qG8kP4q_4BO0wMQUSk2YOJ5ECApBCrVQWIJmXVF1QufVZACyIE8m02AdS4jZM6P7BnPS_8r_WI1rBF$ >

Submission Form

Can you send your information? Please also share the name of Ph.D. students
who may work on this project.

[image: Screenshot 2025-10-22 at 10.00.11 AM.png][image: Screenshot
2025-10-22 at 9.58.57 AM.png]

Best,
Hong

-- 
Hongseok Namkoong
Assistant Professor, Division of Decision, Risk, and Operations
Columbia Business School

Kravis 933, 625 W 130th Street, New York, NY 10027
namkoong@gsb.columbia.edu
hsnamkoong.github.io
